title: Benchmarking Small Language Models and Small Reasoning Language Models on System Log Severity Classification

methodology.focus: Evaluating small, deployable language models for system log severity classification
methodology.evaluation_approach: Using severity classification as a lens for evaluating model competence and real-time deployability
methodology.implications: Root cause analysis (RCA) and broader DT integration

context.challenge: Manual interpretation of logs has become infeasible due to massive volumes and complex language
context.technology: Large language models (LLMs) combined with retrieval-augmented generation (RAG)
context.protocol: Syslog protocol for transmitting event notifications

log_severity.description: Integer that quantifies the risk level of each log
log_severity.standardization: Lacks strict standardization, making it a realistic and challenging probe

syslog_priority.calculation: Facility value (0-23) multiplied by 8 plus Severity value
syslog_priority.purpose: Administrators use to identify and resolve system errors

requirements.digital_twin_pipelines: Log interpretation must be both accurate and latency-efficient
requirements.model_evaluation: Compact, deployable language models under strict output constraints
